message.content:
Review the plan for goal **G11** (hygiene cleanup) in CONFIGURED mode (return verdict JSON, write NOTHING). Read `fetch_item("goals","G11")`, its 7 defects (D40/D45/D51/D54/D56/D57/D60), and tasks T136 (lint-gate D45+D54) → T137 (e2e ports D51) / T138 (config comments D57+D60) / T139 (bind seams D56) / T140 (capability D40), all dependsOn T136, under M47.

Be adversarial per the /cq:plan-review rubric. Ground against .golangci.yml (confirm v2 format → the D54 fix must be linters.exclusions.paths not run.skip-dirs), Justfile, the 3 D45 finding sites, internal/bind/pathsock_linux.go CAP comment + packaging/systemd/wanbond-edge.service, test/e2e ports, internal/config/config.go stale comments (D57 :569-585, D60 :78-81/:488-494), internal/bind/multipath.go read seams. Check: (1) is the T136 v2-config grounding right + does hermeticity (throwaway-file test) + the 3 findings hold? (2) D57/D60 comment locations accurate + is replace-vs-delete right per defect? (3) T139 — is deleting PathSnapshots/FECSnapshot safe (no production callers) + does single-sourcing preserve the delivered-count rule? (4) T140 — is the empirical worker probe the right way to settle the CAP question, and does 'don't widen the bounding set unless proven' correctly avoid a security regression? (5) all-dependsOn-T136 necessary (serializes the two pathsock editors) or excessive for the parallel-safe tasks (T138 config.go, T139 multipath.go, T137 e2e are disjoint files)? (6) Missing scope.

Return JSON ONLY: {"summary": "...", "verdict": "go-ahead"|"revise", "new_questions": [], "criticism": []}. End with a `### Session summary` block.

attachment.content:
- baboon: Baboon data model compiler reference. Covers model syntax, multi-version evolution, code generation, wire-format conversions, database migrations, backward compatibility, and version negotiation. TRIGGER when: designing, changing, or discussing any external API, working with .baboon model files, updating data models, adding new fields or types, bumping model versions, running code generation, implementing conversions, writing database migrations, or discussing API versioning strategy.
- constructive-test-taxonomy: Multi-axis classification of tests (Intention × Encapsulation × Isolation) for evaluating test quality and steering test-writing decisions. Replaces fuzzy Unit/Integration/E2E vocabulary with precise tags and a maintenance-cost heuristic. TRIGGER when: writing new tests, reviewing a test suite, deciding what to test or at what level, discussing mocks vs. real dependencies, classifying tests, or arguing about test coverage or test-strategy tradeoffs.
- dual-tests: Pattern for testing code that touches external systems: write one abstract test suite against an interface, then run it against both the production adapter (real DB/API/FS) and a hand-written in-memory dummy. Prefers manual dummies over auto-generated mocks. TRIGGER when: designing code that depends on a database, HTTP API, message queue, filesystem, or other external system; choosing between mocks, fakes, or dummies; adding integration points; setting up test infrastructure; or when the `constructive-test-taxonomy` skill calls for moving Communication-tier tests toward Blackbox-Atomic.
- environment: Bubblewrap "yolo" sandbox escape workflow (exchange scripts for reaching $HOME, system paths, or files outside the sandbox) plus in-sandbox nix/direnv usage. TRIGGER only when $SMIND_SANDBOXED=1 — i.e. running inside the yolo sandbox and you need to reach something outside it. SKIP entirely when $SMIND_SANDBOXED is unset: you are unsandboxed, so write directly and ignore this skill.
- izumi: Best-practice playbook for the izumi Scala ecosystem — distage (DI), distage-framework (roles/RoleAppMain), distage-testkit (test harness with memoization), distage-framework-docker (test containers), logstage (structured logging), and BIO (bifunctor effect typeclasses). Covers idiomatic bindings, Activation/Axis switching, Lifecycle discipline, plugin layout, and how to apply the `dual-tests` pattern inside distage so one test body runs against both a dummy and a real-DB adapter. TRIGGER when: writing or reviewing Scala code that imports `izumi.distage.*`, `izumi.logstage.*`, `izumi.functional.bio.*`, `distage.*`, `logstage.*`; designing a new distage plugin or Role; wiring a testkit `SpecZIO`/`Spec2`/`Spec1`; configuring `ContainerDef`/`DockerSupportModule`; choosing between `StandardAxis.Repo` or `Scene` tags; or implementing the dual-tests pattern in a distage project. The canonical reference project is https://github.com/7mind/distage-example.
- resilient-ws-ui: Guidelines for building applications that maintain reliable WebSocket connections to a backend and surface connection health clearly to the user. Invoke ONLY when the user explicitly references this skill by name (e.g. "/resilient-ws-ui", "use the resilient-ws-ui skill"). Do NOT auto-invoke based on topic match.
- tass: Target/Actual State Separation (TASS) pattern for reliable state management in event-driven control systems. TRIGGER when: implementing device or entity state management, designing control systems, working with target vs actual state separation, or building event-driven systems with commanded/observed state.
- cq:advance: Advance the WHOLE flow one cycle — chain /cq:investigate:advance, /cq:plan:advance, /cq:implement:advance to quiescence, then report DRAINED / BLOCKED-ON-QUESTIONS / MIXED.
- cq:implement-review: Implement-flow adversarial per-task review rubric + stdout-JSON contract, as a portable promptTemplate for non-Claude harnesses (pi/Codex). Symmetric to cq:plan-review. Judges ONE task's implementation, splits findings into autonomously-fixable criticism[], user-only questions[], and out-of-scope defects[], and emits the verdict as JSON on stdout. Writes NOTHING to the ledger — the orchestrator records the terminal review.
- cq:implement:advance: Advance an implement-flow run one full pass — pick DAG-ready tasks, implement each in an isolated worktree, adversarially review, autonomously fix criticism, register user questions, and merge back in dependency order.
- cq:implement:start: Start an implement-flow run — resolve the target milestones (default: all open ones), validate their task DAG, then hand off to the /cq:implement:advance loop.
- cq:investigate: Start an investigate-flow run — defect intake + bootstrap, then run the /cq:investigate:advance pass inline.
- cq:investigate:advance: Advance an investigate-flow run one research round — read defect state, form/extend the hypothesis tree, dispatch read-only explorers, validate every citation against source, adjudicate node status, and on a confirmed root cause file-and-defer the fix to plan-flow.
- cq:plan: Start a plan-flow goal — create the goal (from free text OR one-or-more idea-ids, one goal per idea), then hand off to the planner for the first clarifying questions.
- cq:plan-review: Shared adversarial plan-review rubric — judge an emitted plan for a goal against the fine-grained / sequenced / testable / grounded / complete rubric, classify findings into new_questions / criticism / defects, and emit a single fenced-json verdict on stdout. The CANONICAL rubric source; Claude's plan-reviewer agent and every non-Claude (Codex/Pi) reviewer judge the same way and emit the same JSON.
- cq:plan:advance: Advance plan-flow goals one full round — a given goal, or (no argument) every unlocked goal — running the planner↔reviewer loop until each needs the user or reaches `planned`.
- cq:plan:follow-up: Add new scope to an EXISTING plan-flow goal — append the follow-up request, re-open the goal, and hand to the planner for a fresh clarifying round.
- cq:planners: Session-only planner-set override — parse a natural-language planner instruction (e.g. "use grok and opus only") into canonical harness:model tokens via the cq.toml [aliases] table (from the ledger MCP get_planners/get_config) plus a documented fallback map; echo the resolved active set; confirm the override is SESSION-ONLY and reverts on the next fresh run.
- cq:reviewers: Session-only reviewer-set override — parse a natural-language reviewer instruction (e.g. "use grok and opus only") into canonical harness:model tokens via the cq.toml [aliases] table (from the ledger MCP get_reviewers/get_config) plus a documented fallback map; echo the resolved active set; confirm the override is SESSION-ONLY and reverts on the next fresh run.
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- codex:rescue: Delegate investigation, an explicit fix request, or follow-up rescue work to the Codex rescue subagent
- codex:setup: Check whether the local Codex CLI is ready and optionally toggle the stop-time review gate
- codex:codex-cli-runtime: Internal helper contract for calling the codex-companion runtime from Claude Code
- codex:codex-result-handling: Internal guidance for presenting Codex helper output back to the user
- codex:gpt-5-4-prompting: Internal guidance for composing Codex and GPT-5.4 prompts for coding, review, diagnosis, and research tasks inside the Codex Claude Code plugin
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

version: "2"

run:
  timeout: 5m

linters:
  # "standard" = errcheck, govet, ineffassign, staticcheck, unused.
  default: standard
  enable:
    - bodyclose
    - errorlint
    - misspell
    - unconvert
  exclusions:
    generated: lax
    rules:
      # Test files may ignore errors from setup helpers.
      - path: _test\.go
        linters:
          - errcheck

formatters:
  enable:
    - gofmt

1	# wanbond task runner. Targets assume the dev shell is active (`nix develop`),
2	# which puts go, golangci-lint, and the netem/DPI tooling on PATH.
3	
4	# Show available targets.
5	default:
6	    @just --list
7	
8	# Build all non-privileged packages.
9	build:
10	    go build ./...
11	
12	# Cross-compile static release binaries (CGO_ENABLED=0, stripped, version
13	# stamped from git) for linux/amd64 and linux/arm64 into dist/. `file` reports
14	# them statically linked; see docs/install.md for the deployment steps.
15	release:
16	    mkdir -p dist
17	    CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux GOARCH=amd64 go build -trimpath -ldflags "-s -w -X main.version=$(git describe --tags --always --dirty)" -o dist/wanbond-linux-amd64 ./cmd/wanbond
18	    CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux GOARCH=arm64 go build -trimpath -ldflags "-s -w -X main.version=$(git describe --tags --always --dirty)" -o dist/wanbond-linux-arm64 ./cmd/wanbond
19	
20	# The e2e/realhosts sources are gated behind //go:build tags, so a tagless
21	# vet/lint never compiles them and silently skips them (see D28). The tagged
22	# passes below are additive — the tagless run still analyses the default build
23	# exactly as before.
24	# Vet + lint the default build plus the e2e- and realhosts-tagged sources.
25	lint:
26	    go vet ./...
27	    go vet -tags e2e ./test/e2e/...
28	    go vet -tags realhosts ./test/realhosts/...
29	    golangci-lint run
30	    golangci-lint run --build-tags e2e ./test/e2e/...
31	    golangci-lint run --build-tags realhosts ./test/realhosts/...
32	
33	# Format check (fails if anything is unformatted).
34	fmt-check:
35	    test -z "$(gofmt -l cmd internal test)"
36	
37	# Unprivileged unit/property tests only (the e2e build tag is never set here, so
38	# no privileged test compiles or runs).
39	test:
40	    go test ./...
41	
42	# Privileged end-to-end suite: netns/netem + TUN tunnel bring-up. Requires root
43	# (CAP_NET_ADMIN + /dev/net/tun); run on real or emulated hardware, not CI.
44	e2e:
45	    sudo -E go test -tags e2e ./test/e2e/... -count=1
46	
47	# Run one named e2e phase test, e.g. `just e2e-run TestP1Failover`.
48	e2e-run TEST:
49	    sudo -E go test -tags e2e ./test/e2e/... -run {{TEST}} -count=1 -v
50	
51	# Opt-in real-host e2e tier: drive the two standing worker machines (edge behind
52	# symmetric NAT, aarch64 concentrator) over SSH. Report-only — it records host
53	# uname/arch and gates nothing. Needs the `llm` SSH key (WANBOND_SSH_KEY, default
54	# /run/agenix/llm-ssh-key); no root required. NEVER part of `just test` or CI.
55	# Run all: `just realhosts`; one test: `just realhosts TestRealConnectivity`.
56	realhosts TEST="":
57	    go test -tags realhosts ./test/realhosts/... {{ if TEST != "" { "-run " + TEST } else { "" } }} -count=1 -v
58	
59	# Opt-in idempotent real-host provisioning: ensure iperf3 + gcc + Go 1.26.x on
60	# both hosts and the concentrator's tunnel-interface INPUT ACCEPT rule. Report-
61	# only, idempotent (a second run reports no changes); same SSH key requirements
62	# as `realhosts`. Thin wrapper over TestRealProvision. NEVER part of `just test`.
63	realhosts-provision:
64	    go test -tags realhosts ./test/realhosts/... -run TestRealProvision -count=1 -v
65	
66	# P0 real-link baseline: the SINGLE repeatable pre-pilot procedure (docs/manual-
67	# checklist.md §P0). A thin orchestration layer over three EXISTING realhosts
68	# tests — it provisions both ends, brings the tunnel up, and records:
69	#   - TestRealP0Smoke              single-uplink handshake + ping RTT + iperf3
70	#                                  (single/8x-parallel TCP, UDP goodput/loss);
71	#   - TestRealAggregationBufferbloat  per-path + bonded throughput and their
72	#                                  aggregation ratio, plus idle-vs-loaded RTT
73	#                                  bufferbloat delta under saturating load;
74	#   - TestRealMidTransferWANKill   mid-transfer LINK failover and HUB failover
75	#                                  (T57) recovery timings.
76	# It TEES the full -v output to a timestamped baseline report under
77	# test/realhosts/reports/ (gitignored). REPORT-ONLY (Q19): the underlying tests
78	# assert LIVENESS ONLY — no Mbit/s or ms threshold gates the run; the numbers are
79	# informational for the operator's (non-blocking) pilot-gate decision. Same SSH
80	# key requirement as `realhosts` (WANBOND_SSH_KEY, default /run/agenix/llm-ssh-
81	# key). NEVER part of `just test` or CI. A non-zero exit means the run itself
82	# could not complete (host unreachable, tunnel never came up), NOT that a
83	# performance number missed a target.
84	p0-baseline:
85	    #!/usr/bin/env bash
86	    set -euo pipefail
87	    mkdir -p test/realhosts/reports
88	    report="test/realhosts/reports/p0-baseline-$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ).log"
89	    echo "wanbond P0 real-link baseline — $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)" | tee "$report"
90	    go test -tags realhosts ./test/realhosts/... \
91	        -run '^(TestRealP0Smoke|TestRealAggregationBufferbloat|TestRealMidTransferWANKill)$' \
92	        -count=1 -v 2>&1 | tee -a "$report"
93	    echo "baseline report: $report" | tee -a "$report"
94

file.content:
# wanbond task runner. Targets assume the dev shell is active (`nix develop`),
# which puts go, golangci-lint, and the netem/DPI tooling on PATH.

# Show available targets.
default:
    @just --list

# Build all non-privileged packages.
build:
    go build ./...

# Cross-compile static release binaries (CGO_ENABLED=0, stripped, version
# stamped from git) for linux/amd64 and linux/arm64 into dist/. `file` reports
# them statically linked; see docs/install.md for the deployment steps.
release:
    mkdir -p dist
    CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux GOARCH=amd64 go build -trimpath -ldflags "-s -w -X main.version=$(git describe --tags --always --dirty)" -o dist/wanbond-linux-amd64 ./cmd/wanbond
    CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux GOARCH=arm64 go build -trimpath -ldflags "-s -w -X main.version=$(git describe --tags --always --dirty)" -o dist/wanbond-linux-arm64 ./cmd/wanbond

# The e2e/realhosts sources are gated behind //go:build tags, so a tagless
# vet/lint never compiles them and silently skips them (see D28). The tagged
# passes below are additive — the tagless run still analyses the default build
# exactly as before.
# Vet + lint the default build plus the e2e- and realhosts-tagged sources.
lint:
    go vet ./...
    go vet -tags e2e ./test/e2e/...
    go vet -tags realhosts ./test/realhosts/...
    golangci-lint run
    golangci-lint run --build-tags e2e ./test/e2e/...
    golangci-lint run --build-tags realhosts ./test/realhosts/...

# Format check (fails if anything is unformatted).
fmt-check:
    test -z "$(gofmt -l cmd internal test)"

# Unprivileged unit/property tests only (the e2e build tag is never set here, so
# no privileged test compiles or runs).
test:
    go test ./...

# Privileged end-to-end suite: netns/netem + TUN tunnel bring-up. Requires root
# (CAP_NET_ADMIN + /dev/net/tun); run on real or emulated hardware, not CI.
e2e:
    sudo -E go test -tags e2e ./test/e2e/... -count=1

# Run one named e2e phase test, e.g. `just e2e-run TestP1Failover`.
e2e-run TEST:
    sudo -E go test -tags e2e ./test/e2e/... -run {{TEST}} -count=1 -v

# Opt-in real-host e2e tier: drive the two standing worker machines (edge behind
# symmetric NAT, aarch64 concentrator) over SSH. Report-only — it records host
# uname/arch and gates nothing. Needs the `llm` SSH key (WANBOND_SSH_KEY, default
# /run/agenix/llm-ssh-key); no root required. NEVER part of `just test` or CI.
# Run all: `just realhosts`; one test: `just realhosts TestRealConnectivity`.
realhosts TEST="":
    go test -tags realhosts ./test/realhosts/... {{ if TEST != "" { "-run " + TEST } else { "" } }} -count=1 -v

# Opt-in idempotent real-host provisioning: ensure iperf3 + gcc + Go 1.26.x on
# both hosts and the concentrator's tunnel-interface INPUT ACCEPT rule. Report-
# only, idempotent (a second run reports no changes); same SSH key requirements
# as `realhosts`. Thin wrapper over TestRealProvision. NEVER part of `just test`.
realhosts-provision:
    go test -tags realhosts ./test/realhosts/... -run TestRealProvision -count=1 -v

# P0 real-link baseline: the SINGLE repeatable pre-pilot procedure (docs/manual-
# checklist.md §P0). A thin orchestration layer over three EXISTING realhosts
# tests — it provisions both ends, brings the tunnel up, and records:
#   - TestRealP0Smoke              single-uplink handshake + ping RTT + iperf3
#                                  (single/8x-parallel TCP, UDP goodput/loss);
#   - TestRealAggregationBufferbloat  per-path + bonded throughput and their
#                                  aggregation ratio, plus idle-vs-loaded RTT
#                                  bufferbloat delta under saturating load;
#   - TestRealMidTransferWANKill   mid-transfer LINK failover and HUB failover
#                                  (T57) recovery timings.
# It TEES the full -v output to a timestamped baseline report under
# test/realhosts/reports/ (gitignored). REPORT-ONLY (Q19): the underlying tests
# assert LIVENESS ONLY — no Mbit/s or ms threshold gates the run; the numbers are
# informational for the operator's (non-blocking) pilot-gate decision. Same SSH
# key requirement as `realhosts` (WANBOND_SSH_KEY, default /run/agenix/llm-ssh-
# key). NEVER part of `just test` or CI. A non-zero exit means the run itself
# could not complete (host unreachable, tunnel never came up), NOT that a
# performance number missed a target.
p0-baseline:
    #!/usr/bin/env bash
    set -euo pipefail
    mkdir -p test/realhosts/reports
    report="test/realhosts/reports/p0-baseline-$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ).log"
    echo "wanbond P0 real-link baseline — $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)" | tee "$report"
    go test -tags realhosts ./test/realhosts/... \
        -run '^(TestRealP0Smoke|TestRealAggregationBufferbloat|TestRealMidTransferWANKill)$' \
        -count=1 -v 2>&1 | tee -a "$report"
    echo "baseline report: $report" | tee -a "$report"

174	// the decoder's shards match what the encoder coded.
175	func fecShardPayload(seq uint64, inner []byte) []byte {
176		b := make([]byte, fecSeqPrefixLen+len(inner))
177		binary.BigEndian.PutUint64(b, seq)

183	// copy of the inner datagram. It fails fast on a shard too short to hold the prefix,

185	// condition. The inner copy lets the resequencer take ownership without retaining the
186	// larger coded-shard backing array.

17	// The fixed-ratio Reed-Solomon engine (internal/fec) is wired into the multipath
18	// datapath as a SEND-side encoder and a RECEIVE-side decoder that composes with the
19	// T18 resequencer, T21 weighted scheduling, and T30 dynamic paths without regressing
20	// any of them. FEC is OFF unless a config carries an [fec] block (NewMultipath's
21	// fecCfg is nil otherwise), in which case the datapath is byte-for-byte the pre-T24
22	// behaviour.

24	// Wire mapping. The FEC data-shard bytes are OuterSeq || inner: the encoder codes
25	// parity over that, but the 8-byte outer-seq prefix is NOT transmitted — the DATA
26	// frame carries the outer-seq in its header and the inner datagram in its payload,
27	// and both sender and receiver reconstitute the shard bytes as
28	// header-seq || frame-payload. This keeps a recovered shard SELF-DESCRIBING (its
29	// reconstructed bytes carry its own outer-seq, so a frame reconstructed from parity
30	// resequences at its ORIGINAL outer-seq even when EVERY data frame of the group was
31	// lost) while adding only one wire byte per DATA frame (the shard index in the DATA
32	// header) and the group cardinality M per PARITY frame. See internal/frame.
33	//

35	// a single deadline-tick goroutine that TryLocks m.mu (never blocking, so it can
36	// never deadlock Close's readersWG.Wait held under m.mu — the tickLivenessFromReceive

38	// concurrent), so it is guarded by its own mutex, disjoint from m.mu and from the
39	// resequencer's — matching the resequencer's lock discipline (never held across a
40	// syscall). Recovered frames are handed to the resequencer exactly like a
41	// natively-received frame, so FEC recovery slots in strictly BEFORE resequencing.

44	// FEC data-shard's coded bytes. It is reconstructed from the DATA frame header on
45	// both ends and never travels the wire (see the wire mapping above).

53	// gap is skipped first and the recovered frame dropped as past the release point. Held
54	// at half the resequencer timeout, the deadline leaves the other half for propagation

126		// synchronized (its own mutex), fed concurrently by the per-path readLoop goroutines

132	// reconstructed (never-received frames). It holds the decoder mutex only for the
133	// in-memory reconstruct — never across a syscall — so it never blocks the send path.
134	func (r *fecReceiver) offer(s fec.Shard) ([]fec.Recovered, error) {
135		r.mu.Lock()
136		defer r.mu.Unlock()
137		return r.dec.Offer(s)
138	}

141	func (r *fecReceiver) stats() fec.DecoderStats {
142		r.mu.Lock()
143		defer r.mu.Unlock()
144		return r.dec.Stats()
145	}

147	// FECStats is a consistent snapshot of the Bind's FEC counters (T24), the shape the
148	// metrics.Source adapter maps onto the connection-scoped /metrics FEC series.
149	// DataFrames is the send-side DATA-frame count (the fixed-ratio overhead's
150	// denominator); ParityFrames/ParityBytes are the send-side overhead (parity emitted);
151	// Recovered and Unrecoverable are the receive-side outcome (data shards reconstructed
152	// from parity, and data shards lost beyond repair capacity). FEC being
153	// connection-scoped, these carry no path label. The frame overhead the operator cares
154	// about is ParityFrames/DataFrames, which tends to ParityShards/DataShards (M/K) once
155	// groups fill; both are counted only for frames that actually reached the socket, so
156	// the ratio reflects wire cost actually spent.
157	type FECStats struct {
158		DataFrames    uint64
159		DataBytes     uint64
160		ParityFrames  uint64
161		ParityBytes   uint64
162		Recovered     uint64
163		Unrecoverable uint64
164		// ResidualLoss is the current post-FEC-recovery connection loss fraction in [0,1]
165		// (T29): the share of outer-seqs neither natively received nor reconstructed from
166		// parity. It is the P4 acceptance signal — the loss FEC did not mask. Zero when FEC is
167		// disabled or no data has flowed.
168		ResidualLoss float64
169	}

171	// fecShardPayload builds the FEC data-shard coded bytes for outer-seq seq over the
172	// opaque inner datagram: seq (8 BE) || inner. The sender codes parity over this; the
173	// receiver reconstitutes the identical bytes from the DATA frame header + payload so
174	// the decoder's shards match what the encoder coded.
175	func fecShardPayload(seq uint64, inner []byte) []byte {
176		b := make([]byte, fecSeqPrefixLen+len(inner))
177		binary.BigEndian.PutUint64(b, seq)
178		copy(b[fecSeqPrefixLen:], inner)
179		return b
180	}
181	
182	// splitFECShardPayload inverts fecShardPayload, returning the outer-seq and a fresh
183	// copy of the inner datagram. It fails fast on a shard too short to hold the prefix,
184	// which would indicate a decoder/encoder mismatch rather than a recoverable
185	// condition. The inner copy lets the resequencer take ownership without retaining the
186	// larger coded-shard backing array.
187	func splitFECShardPayload(b []byte) (uint64, []byte, error) {
188		if len(b) < fecSeqPrefixLen {
189			return 0, nil, fmt.Errorf("bind: recovered FEC shard %d bytes, shorter than the %d-byte outer-seq prefix", len(b), fecSeqPrefixLen)
190		}
191		seq := binary.BigEndian.Uint64(b[:fecSeqPrefixLen])
192		inner := make([]byte, len(b)-fecSeqPrefixLen)
193		copy(inner, b[fecSeqPrefixLen:])
194		return seq, inner, nil
195	}

ConditionPathExists=/etc/wanbond/edge.toml

[Service]
Type=exec
ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/wanbond --config /etc/wanbond/edge.toml
# SIGHUP re-reads the config and applies the path diff (add/remove uplinks)
# without tearing down the live tunnel.
ExecReload=/bin/kill -HUP $MAINPID
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=2s

# The daemon needs only CAP_NET_ADMIN (TUN device creation); it does no
# interface addressing or routing itself (see docs/install.md).
CapabilityBoundingSet=CAP_NET_ADMIN
DeviceAllow=/dev/net/tun rw
NoNewPrivileges=yes
ProtectSystem=strict
ProtectHome=yes
PrivateTmp=yes
ProtectKernelTunables=yes
ProtectKernelModules=yes
ProtectKernelLogs=yes
ProtectControlGroups=yes
ProtectClock=yes
ProtectHostname=yes
RestrictAddressFamilies=AF_INET AF_INET6 AF_NETLINK AF_UNIX
RestrictNamespaces=yes
RestrictRealtime=yes
RestrictSUIDSGID=yes
LockPersonality=yes
MemoryDenyWriteExecute=yes
SystemCallArchitectures=native
SystemCallFilter=@system-service
UMask=0077

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target

(The `:9090` hits at `p3_fec_test.go:488` and `p4_adaptive_test.go:383` are prose in doc-string curl examples, not listen addresses. `multipeer_test.go:129` is a comment noting "the current max is 9101".)

- `internal/bind/pathsock_linux.go:9` — `// currently assigned to it. It requires CAP_NET_RAW; a permission error is`
- `test/e2e/wireaudit_test.go:326` — `// CAP_NET_RAW (the e2e TestMain namespace / sudo provides it). It uses tcpdump's`

Also referenced in planning docs (not code): `wanbond-fixes.md:61-65` (D6: code says CAP_NET_RAW, shipped unit grants CAP_NET_ADMIN) and `.cq/goals.md:189,296` (D40, same mismatch).

## 3. PathSnapshots / FECSnapshot references

**Definitions (bind Multipath methods) — internal/bind/multipath.go:**
- `2085` — `func (m *Multipath) FECSnapshot() FECStats`
- `2701` — `func (m *Multipath) PathSnapshots() []PathTraffic`

**Unrelated STRUCT type (metrics package) — internal/metrics/metrics.go:**
- `109` — `type FECSnapshot struct` (used in `internal/metrics/metrics_test.go:24,31,226,255,298,343` and via `FEC() []FECSnapshot` at `202`; these are the struct, NOT the bind method).

**Test callers of the bind METHODS (all inside internal/bind):**
- `internal/bind/traffic_test.go` — `m.PathSnapshots()` at lines **45, 97, 112** = **3 callers**
- `internal/bind/fec_test.go` — `sender/rx.FECSnapshot()` at lines **239, 338, 425, 492, 584, 663** = **6 callers**

**Callers outside internal/bind:** NONE. Grep for `.PathSnapshots()`/`.FECSnapshot()` in non-test source returned nothing. Production (`internal/device/metrics.go:19`) consumes `PeerSnapshots()` instead. The `internal/device/metrics.go:15,115,117,120` and `internal/device/metrics_test.go:95,263` hits are all the **metrics.FECSnapshot STRUCT**, not the bind method. `test/e2e/tolerant_startup_test.go:90` is only a comment mentioning the pre-T94 name. So the superseded bind read seams are referenced exclusively by their own bind unit tests.

## 4. "mirrors" comment near PeerSnapshots

`grep -n "mirrors"` gives three hits (471, 2157, 2694). The one adjacent to the snapshot implementations is `internal/bind/multipath.go:2694`, in the PathSnapshots doc-comment (PeerSnapshots is defined right after at 2728):

> `internal/bind/multipath.go:2693-2695`
> "...then RELEASES m.mu BEFORE calling / the (independently-synchronized) prober's Estimate()/State(). This **mirrors** / tickLivenessFromReceive's discipline..."

Also note the derivation comment at `multipath.go:2796`: "// Mirrors FECSnapshot's Recovered/Unrecoverable derivation verbatim" (capital "Mirrors", within the PeerSnapshots body).

## 5. staticcheck QF1001 candidate in pathsock.go

Single `!(` hit, line 242:

```
internal/bind/pathsock.go
241		ip = ip.Unmap()
242		if ip.Is4() == want.Is4() && !(excludeLinkLocal && ip.IsLinkLocalUnicast()) {
243			familyCount++
```

Total line count: **279 lines** (`internal/bind/pathsock.go`).

## 6. selectDeviceBinds switches on config bind mode — confirmed

`internal/bind/pathsock.go`:
- `115` — `func selectDeviceBinds(srcs []netip.Addr, modes []config.BindMode, resolve func(netip.Addr) ifaceInfo) []string`
- `128` — `case config.BindModeSource:`
- `131` — `case config.BindModeDevice:`
- `136` — `default: // config.BindModeAuto, or any other/unset value.`
- `71` — `planPathBinds` delegates: `return selectDeviceBinds(srcs, modes, ...)`

Confirmed: `selectDeviceBinds` is a `switch` over `config.BindMode` (Source / Device / default=Auto).

## 7. packaging/systemd/ concentrator unit + CAP_NET

`packaging/systemd/` contains three unit files:
- `wanbond-addressing@.service`
- `wanbond-concentrator.service` ← **yes, a concentrator unit exists**
- `wanbond-edge.service`

`grep -n "CAP_NET"`:
- `wanbond-edge.service:22` — `# ...needs only CAP_NET_ADMIN (TUN device creation)...`
- `wanbond-edge.service:24` — `CapabilityBoundingSet=CAP_NET_ADMIN`
- `wanbond-concentrator.service:27` — `# ...needs only CAP_NET_ADMIN (TUN device creation)...`
- `wanbond-concentrator.service:29` — `# CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE here only if wireguard.listen_port is below 1024.`
- `wanbond-concentrator.service:30` — `CapabilityBoundingSet=CAP_NET_ADMIN`

(`wanbond-addressing@.service` has no CAP_NET line.) This is the D6/D40 mismatch: units grant `CAP_NET_ADMIN` only, while the pathsock code comments claim `CAP_NET_RAW` is required.

## 8. internal/device/ referencing the bind seams

`grep -rn "PathSnapshots\|FECSnapshot" internal/device/`:
- `internal/device/metrics.go:15` — comment only ("pre-T94 PathSnapshots/FECSnapshot (primary-peer-only)")
- `internal/device/metrics.go:115,117,120` — `metrics.FECSnapshot` **struct** (the exposition type)
- `internal/device/metrics_test.go:95,263` — `metrics.FECSnapshot` **struct**

Device tests do NOT call the bind `PathSnapshots()`/`FECSnapshot()` methods; production/device code migrated to `PeerSnapshots()` (`internal/device/metrics.go:19`). The only remaining mentions are the metrics struct and a historical comment.
